desc:
The weeds grow wild here, blocking movement towards a small farm just to the
east. The farmer uses this place to dump all the weeds and other plants he has
removed from his farm. After some months, the cuttings will fully decompose and
be used as fertilizer in the fields.
name: Compost Heap

desc:
This is the southern part of a field belonging to the farm that stand just east
of here. The furrows are well-cultivated, as it usual in Shire. There is a
strange heap in the east.
name: A Field not Far from a Farm

desc:
Yuck! The land is all full of fresh mud here, nothing strange since this is
where the pigs live. They seem to be happy though. North you see some fields of
the farm and this place has no other exit.
name: A Pig Pen

desc:
Here is where the workers of the farm seem to put their tools and cultivation
implements. Hobbits are not too methodical from what you can see.
name: Tool Shed

desc:
Here is where the farmer lives. It is a nice place if you don't hit the ceiling
with your head. You can take a look outside through the rounded windows.
Everything seems small and charming. You can go to the kitchen to the east or to
the bedroom to the south.
name: Hobbit House

desc:
The smell of food reaches your nose and you suddenly realise how hungry you are!
Hobbits have always been known for their culinary arts, and this kitchen proves
it! On a table you can see they are not too methodical though, since it is
covered with dirty dishes. This is not a clean family.
name: Kitchen

desc:
Hobbits like to eat, and for that they need good food. In this field you can see
what they mean by that. West is the entrance to a hobbit house, while to the
north the farm continues and east you see some of the grassy fields of the
Shire.
name: Furrowed Fields

desc:
Everything around is an immense field of thick grass. You can see some lonely
trees growing in the middle. To the west you can see a hobbit farm and its
cultivated fields. Colorful flowers add life to the scene.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are now on the side of the wide path that goes to Stock, a small hobbit
village to the east. The land is covered by thick grass and some trees, flowers
and even holes that make it hard to walk here. Better take the path.
name: Side of the Path

desc:
You are now on the side of the wide path that goes to Stock, a small hobbit
village to the east. The land is covered by thick grass and some trees, flowers
and even holes that make it hard to walk here. Better take the path.
name: Side of the Path

desc:
Everything is small and nice in here! A bed on one side of the room invites you
to take a nap, but if you are not a hobbit it could break! Round windows on the
walls let you see the outside and some pictures show the members of the family.
name: Bedroom

desc:
It seems that this is a path less well travelled than many in the Shire, as
patches of grass can be seen growing here and there upon it. The path itself
travels due east to west here, and to the south it is bordered by dense and
impassable undergrowth. To the north you might choose to step off the path, and
travel in the denser grass.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The grass softly moves on the ground has you walk this dusty little road. Small
groves here and there and the general roughness of the surrounding provide many
places to hide. But no danger seems to lurk in such places, everything is quiet,
and that small town called Stock is not too far to the east.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The peace of this land can be felt in the air. The only sound you hear is that
of the little animals that dwell the grass and the flowers. Around you the
meadow is mostly that, but some trees grow solitary in the uneven ground. You
are now taking a road crossing the Shire. To the east you know you'll find the
village of Stock, but to the southwest a mysterious forest rises.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The Stock Road is heading towards the Woody End Forest. As you look towards the
forest, you recall the stories you heard, as a child, about ghosts and long
lost, mysterious treasures. The road continues west and north.
name: Bend in Stock Road

desc:
You blink your eyes to keep out the dust kicked up by your boots. To the south,
the road continues to a crossing in the road. To the east, the road continues
its seemingly endless journey to Stock.
name: Bend in Stock Road

desc:
You have come to a crossing in the road. A road leads east to where you can hear
Hobbits laughing and having a merry time. To the east is the village of
Woodhall. Even if you are just passing through, you should stop by Woodhall and
sample the famous beer of the East Farthing. Stock Road continues to the north
and west.
name: Stock Road Crossing

desc:
You decide that you should be more careful as you stumble and nearly fall in a
rut in the road. Your boots continue to raise dust, making it hard to see. The
road continues on to the south and to the east, where you can see a crossing.
name: Bend in Stock Road

desc:
More stone than wood seems to make up these woods. Much of the wood present has
stood untouched for so many ages it has petrified. There is a notable lack of
animal movement or any evidence that animals even pass through this area in
travels to more hospitable land.
name: Stony Woods

desc:
The main point of this oak forest is the silence. There is no bird songs that
can be heared. The trees grow very close one from the other, building a grey
wall in the east. To the west and the south, more space can be seen between
them.
name: A Still Forest

desc:
This field is spongious. In fact, it is soaked by water. Therefore lots of
tracks can be seen in the fresh furrowed earth, showing the intense wild life of
this country: one can even recognise some foxes footprints among the numerous
tracks.
name: Muddy Field

desc:
You are in the general store of the village of Woodhall. A well-built counter
displays the wares that the grocer has for sale today.
name: General Store

desc:
To the east, one can often hear the joyous laughter of hobbits seemingly living
their innocent lives unaware of the evils of the world. In that direction lies
the small village of Woodhall while to the west one would find the Stock Road.
name: A Path to Woodhall

desc:
The air is quiet, but you know the fields hereabouts must be full of life. Small
creatures hide in the grasslands beside the trail, intent upon evading notice by
those who might hunt them. And so unkempt is the land that one would not need to
travel too far from the path before being lost from view.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The road is surrounded by a grass that reaches your knees. Silently, this land
is worthy of its fame of being a peaceful place. You hardly can see signs of
life around you, excepting the road that far to the north you barely see.
Anyway, you know that the fields are full of life. The small village of Stock
rises not too far to the east.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The small river flows down to the Baranduin. After this eastward bend of the
brook the riverbad widens and the current slows down. The banks of the brook are
to slippery to leave the water. The village of Stock is not far to the north.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
The brook bends from the west to the north. The riverbanks are steep and covered
with wet and slippery moss, it is impossible to leave the waterway. The
surrounding landscape is peaceful with a small village to the northeast.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
From the west, the bend of the brook opens up to the east and suddenly, it is
all peaceful again between the wide banks. In the northeast direction, the
outline of a town can be seen.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
The current grows faster as it rounds a bend to the east. The once calm brook
has become much more turbulent, making navigation difficult.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
The brook flows faster here and makes a sharp bend northwards. Its bottom is
narrow and its bed smaller, causing the current to quicken to a dangerous pace.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
The small gentle river flows towards the Baranduin. Its banks are steeper in
this part of it and covered by thick patches of slippery moss making it
impossible to climb on land. The current of the brook is moderate.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
The Stockbrook is a small gentle river. Its mossy banks are lined with tall
alders and willows forming a green ceiling over the clear water. In this part of
the brook, the current is moderate.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
The Stockbrook flows calmly and quietly between the two green banks on either
side. Its crystal clear water allows one to see the bottom of the brook and all
the creatures that dwell within.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
Here, the brook yields to the local terrain and curves from south to east. The
current of the small river towards the Brandywine gains a little in strength and
makes travelling upwards the brook difficult.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
The Stockbrook flows swifly between steep banks. The strong current looks as if
it makes travel downstream very easy. The riverbanks are lined with alders and
willow trees which often times cover the river in shade.
name: Bend in the Brook

desc:
You are standing in the central square of a small Hobbit village named Woodhall.
This village is not as famous as Stock, but it is no less merrier than any other
village your travels have taken you to. To the east is a bar where you can
sample some of the famous East Farthing beer. To the north is a general store
where local Hobbits buy their supplies. A path leads west to Stock Road.
name: Village of Woodhall

desc:
Though quite small, this bar seems to be one well patronised by the local folk,
and rightly so too, for the ales of the Eastfarthing have a great reputation
throughout the Shire. A short, sturdy wooden bar stands at one end of the room,
and a cosy hearth is set into the wall across from the doorway, to warm the room
on cold winter evenings.
name: The Taproom of the Tipsy Cat Tavern

desc:
This plain road is starting to get on your nerves. The dust raised by your boots
gets in your eyes, making it hard to see where you are going. To the south, you
can see the beginning of the Stockbrook. To the north and west, the seemingly
endless road continues.
name: Bend in Stock Road

desc:
The plain road is starting to get on your nerves. The dust raised by your boots
has filled your eyes and it is hard to see where you are going. There is an
archway visible to the northwest.
name: Stock Road

desc:
Here is the beginning of the Stockbrook. Multiple small waterways come from the
higher hills around to flow together to create the Stockbrook. It is an
inspiring sight to see and hear nature hard at work. If your destination is the
River Baranduin, also known as the Brandywine, this seems to be the correct
brook to follow.
name: The Source of Stockbrook

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are on a path that is a shortcut through the forest of Woody End. Many elm
trees tower above your head, keeping out the light. There isn't any underbrush,
but it is still very hard to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the
elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you see trees and more trees. You begin to
wonder if it is true that shortcuts make long delays.
name: A Shortcut

desc:
You are in the middle of a field cultivated to grow turnips. To the east, you
can see trees surrounding the walls of a farm. To the west, you see a path used
as a shortcut through the Woody End forest.
name: A Turnip Field

desc:
You are on a lane that runs east-west between a field and the Causeway. Just
beyond the field to the west is the Woody End forest. To the north is the gate
leading to Old Maggot's farm.
name: Maggot's Lane

desc:
This field, one among hundreds to be found on the plains of the Shire, which is
bounded in part by the Old East Road and the woody hills far to the south, is
farmed and harvested with great care. For only the best wheat can be used to
make the bread that the Hobbits will eat.
name: A Wheat Field

desc:
An important component of a hobbit's diet is the potato. This field, which the
hobbits use to grow potatos, is the largest of all the hobbits' fields. The
furrows run towards the south as far as the eye can see. Perhaps this field
extends as far as the southern hills which are barely visible from here.
name: North of a Potato Field

desc:
Around you are many acres of lush farmland, rows upon rows of cabbages, turnips
and parsnips. The heads of these vegetables cover the area like a perfect green
carpet spreading out in front of you and into the horizon. To the west, the
farmland continues.
name: Lush Farmland

desc:
You are surrounded by beautiful hills that are being used as a pasture for many
animals. Lush green grass grows all around. To the north, you can see some
cultivated land, the west, an orchard, and to the south, the pasture continues.
name: Rolling Meadows

desc:
This field extends as far as the eye can see. It is evident from the evenly
spaced furrows that the field is used to grow corn and other vegetables. While,
there are no signs of the farmers, from the freshness of the furrows it is
obvious that the farmers have not strayed far.
name: Well-farmed Field

desc:
This field is really quite large. While most of the fields in the Shire are of
similar size and are relatively small, this one is so big that one can't see its
northern end from here. There is another field towards the south, where some
woody hills appear, limiting the cultivated plain you are standing on.
name: South of a Potato Field

desc:
Like most fields of the Shire, this one is cultivated. One can't determine what
sort of plants the farmer intends to grow here, because the field was recently
tilled. South of here a forest grows on the large hill which blocks the vision
in that direction.
name: A Furrowed Field

desc:
A path starts here at the core of the forest and seems to lead towards the
north. All around trees can be seen announcing a large forest southwards. To the
west furrowed ground can be found, marking the beginning of some fields.
name: A Track

desc:
As you enter this pasture a calm and sense of peace beckons to you. Even though
this place may be calming there is still an eerie surround to it, things may be
at work here. You notice, in a southerly direction, some huge trees. A barely
visible road lies also southerly from here.
name: Grassland

desc:
To the north and east there is a little forest and another expanse of barren
lands. Several trees are growing randomly within this vicinity. The trees are
healthy looking and seems to be well taken care of. To the north is a small open
forest with several types of trees in. Southerly there is a small trail leading
from a much wider track.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
You walk in a forest, growing in and around a range of hills. All around you
there are signs of life. Travel is rather peaceful. Here, the hills seem to be
dying down, however, only a faint memory, not far east.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You are walking through a range of low, sloping hills. Sparse trees cover the
land. Wild flowers are in bloom. To the south and east, the forest gets much
thicker.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
You are within a fine and well spaced forest. There are some scurrying animals
sniffing along the ground and sniffing the berries which grow here. You can hear
the birds singing on the birches of the trees, the edges of the forest are
visible so you hope it is easier to get out of this forest than some others that
are within this vicinity.
name: Clear Forest

desc:
Stock Road continues its journey through the forest, and enters Tuckborough,
just west of you. The forest is calm, but not quiet. Rather, it is teeming with
life.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
The road continues through the calm forests. The sides of the road are rustling
with action. You wonder if it is wise to camp too close to the woods which
border the road. As you walk on you notice the soft noises of the animals. The
road continues easterly and westerly, to the north is a small expanse of
grassland.
name: Stock Road

desc:
You walk in a forest growing in and around a range of hills, and there are signs
of life everywhere. Travel is rather peaceful, so you relax a bit. The sounds of
a small town reaches your ears just as you spot the wall of what seems to be a
barn just west of here.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The birch, maple, and pine all make an impression in these woods. Your foot
crunches upon the leaves and needles which make up the forest floor. Shafts of
sunlight break through the branches high above, and a soft breeze plays around
you. A quiet, gentle, woods you think, until you hear a dark howl to the south,
and then you wonder.
name: The Woodlands

desc:
A long wooden table occupies nearly all the space within this room. Two long
benches sit beside it. A meal was eaten here rather recently. The dirty
silverware is clear evidence.
name: Pipeweed Smugglers' Dining Hall

desc:
This small room contains a shelf with all the various tools and ingredients
necessary for preparing a large meal. A big pot hangs over a blazing fire. A
small hole in the ceiling allows the smoke to leave the room.
name: Kitchen and Supplies Store

desc:
Three beds stand in this room, one against each wall, save the northern. The
beds are unmade, matching the floor, which is covered by clothes, bedding, and
other litter. The residents are not as orderly as most of the hobbits of the
Shire, that is certain.
name: Sleeping Room

desc:
You pull the curtain aside, and enter what seems to be the living quarters of a
single person. A bed rests alongside the east wall, and a desk is along the
south. On the desk is a letter.
name: Room of the Smugglers' Boss

desc:
The ground is spongy and walking is difficult as it slightly shifts under the
slightest weight. It is covered with wet vegetation, though in parts standing
water can be seen. Marsh gases slowly escape the rotting vegetation, giving the
area a very distinct smell. A trail lies to the east of this bog.
name: The Marish

desc:
The road you are on could not be more mundane than it already is. You notice a
small bog to the west. As you move on your nose slowly starts to recognise the
sweet odours of pipe-weed in the wind. There could be some hobbits close by.
name: Woody Path

desc:
At this point along the road it is impossible to see any entrance to the woods.
All there seems to be is the road you are currently on and the bushes surround
it. There is, however, a small bog to the southwest.
name: Woody Path

desc:
You are standing on the ruins of a once great wooden building. The remains of
the various rooms are scattered all over the area. There is very little left of
the eastern side wall of the building which confuses you. You can return back to
the clearing.
name: A Rotten Hut

desc:
You stand on a top of a beautiful small hill, it is covered with shallow green
grass. You can admire the forest to the north and see the paths going around it.
You can also notice the trunk through which you got here.
name: Green Hills

desc:
You are walking along a very worn path around the woods. The path has once been
a popular way around the woods but nowadays the stone paving is very wretched.
You can see a huge beech trunk to the south.
name: A Wretched Path

desc:
You have entered a small one room hut. Though no one is here, you recognise the
signs of recent habitation.
name: A Tree House

desc:
You are walking on a stone paved path which must have been a larger road at
times when people had affairs that required passing the forest of Woody End.
From here following the path gets a bit nasty, the next step the way to go might
not be exactly clear.
name: A Wretched Path

desc:
You notice the massiveness of the forest from here; the huge trees that form the
forest are growing tightly together. It seems as if going in there would drain
all your energy. You can hear the howling of some foul beasts from the forest
and it freezes your blood.
name: South of the Forest

desc:
You are standing on a small clearing in the southern side of the woods. The path
comfortably leads you here, maybe too comfortably. You notice the prints of
various animals on the path and around the pond here. The old stone paved path
goes on toward east and is engulfed by the forest at some point.
name: A Clearing on the Path

desc:
You have found the secret hide-out of the infamous Woody End Brewers. They are a
party of all races who seek huge profits by selling beers and wines to the
people who cannot go to the cities to buy it. The floor is flooded with beer.
name: The Flooded Basement

desc:
You are walking around in the dense forest of Woody End. Your sense of direction
has disappeared since you entered these woods. There is no way to tell which
direction would be best to go to right now. It may be time to use your
navigational skills.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
The sticky, stinking muddy path seems alive with all manner of small insects.
Mostly they slip by unseen, but they are felt and heard, lending a decidedly
unappealing air to this place. To the north and west lie heavy woodland, where
the ground appears to be firmer. The path stretches to the east. But south of
here the trail peters out, the way blocked by a thick and slimy mire, which may
well prove to be impassable.
name: On the Muddy Path

desc:
You stand in the famous forest of Woody End, many trees around you blocking your
view to few pathetic paces. Most of the trees are oaks and beeches, and heavy
vegetation covers the ground. The humidity is so intense that you could almost
touch it.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You've found the old lonesome tanner's hiding place deep within the woods. He
doesn't seem aggressive; actually he might even be delighted to see other
people, though he isn't going to allow you to stay around for too long. There
are skins of different animals on the ground around him, he seems to be knitting
something from one of them.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
Bustling with life, this forest is very dense; filled with vines and shrubbery.
More woods spread out in all directions, but through the undergrowth a small
path can be glimpsed every now and then.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You have entered the woods from the northern section. The forests are full of
life and of course the feelings of danger are very strong around you. Pairs of
invisible eyes are glaring at you pondering to find the best possible place and
time to make a dinner out of you.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You have entered the woods from the northern section. The forests are full of
life and of course the feelings of danger are very strong around you. Pairs of
invisible eyes are glaring at you pondering to find the best possible place and
time to make a dinner out of you.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are now in the deep forest and wishing that you had not done that. There are
howls and screeches of various animals echoing around. The woods are crawling
with normal forest life such as wolves and other foul beasts. It may be wise for
you to keep your mind sharp and your arms strong. The dark forest continues
towards the east.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are now in the deep forest and wishing that you had not done that. There are
howls and screeches of various animals echoing around. The woods are crawling
with normal forest life such as wolves and other foul beasts. It may be wise for
you to keep your mind sharp and your arms strong. The dark forest continues
towards the east.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the famous forest of Woody End. There are many trees around you which
block and limit your view to within a few paces. Most of the trees are oaks and
beeches, heavy vegetation is apparent on the floor and the density of the
humidity is such that you sense you could almost touch it.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You stand in the famous forest of Woody End. Many trees are around you thus
blocking you view to only a few pathetic paces. Most of the trees are oaks and
beeches. There is a heavy amount of undergrowth and the humidity is so dense you
feel you could almost touch it.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You stand on a small pasture field between the path and the forest. The grass
grows high and rich here. Various insects are seeking for nourishment here. The
Woody End Forest looms to the east and south.
name: Border of the Forest

desc:
You stand on a narrow path going through the forest, or around it. You could not
figure out which it was. Oaks, beeches and other great trees border the path.
Old fallen branches lay on the ground. Now and again you notice something in the
corner of your eye, however, when you turn or look you notice nothing.
name: Narrow Path in the Woody End

desc:
This little clearing is paved by pebbled stones of various kinds which makes
moving some effort. The little stones roll under your feet and make it tougher
to move easily. This kind of terrain wears one out quickly. It seems though that
the stones are limited to this narrow area.
name: Between the Woody End and a Road

desc:
The narrow path travels due north to south here, and apart from the faint noises
made by small creatures hidden in the undergrowth, the only sound to be heard is
the soft sigh of the boughs of the ancient trees which grow all about you.
name: Woody Path

desc:
You find yourself in the shade of a large tree, which stands beside a trail
through the forest. To the south you can see that the trail meets at a woody
intersection, but to the west the woodland grows dense and strong.
name: On the Side of the Path

desc:
The birch, maple and pine trees all make an impression in these woods. Your
movement creates a rustling effect due to all the leaves and needles, lying
along the floor, which your feet are crunching.
name: The Woodlands

desc:
The birch, maple, and pine all make an impression in these woods. Your foot
crunches upon the leaves and needles which make up the forest floor. Shafts of
sunlight break through the branches high above, and a soft breeze plays around
you. A quiet, gentle, woods you think, until you hear a dark howl to the south,
and then you wonder.
name: The Woodlands

desc:
You walk in a forest, growing in and around a range of hills. All around you
there are signs of life. Travel is rather peaceful. Here, the hills seem to be
dying down, however, only a faint memory, not far east.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You walk in a forest, growing in and around a range of hills. All around you
there are signs of life. Travel is rather peaceful. Here, the hills seem to be
dying down, however, only a faint memory, not far east.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
The birch, maple and pine trees all make an impression in these woods. Your
movement crunches creates a rustling effect due to all the leaves and needles
which lay along the floor. After several moves you hear a fading squelching
sound under several of the leaves but you dismiss it.
name: The Woodlands

desc:
The small road you're standing on isn't much of a road after all. Small bushes
of grass grow here and there and the whole road is turning into a brush itself.
The path seems to be fading because of the various plants forcing themselves up
from the hardened surface of the path. It may be possible to suggest that this
place is not often visited.
name: Woody Road

desc:
You are currently travelling along a small path or track. The forests seem to
grow darker and darker as you get closer to the higher woods starting in the
southeast.
name: Woody Road

desc:
The hill is beautifully covered by green grass and some small plants. There do
not seem to be any flowers though. Due to the height of the hill compared to its
surroundings, you get a better view of the area around you. There are the woods
and the two roads, one to the north and one to the west of here.
name: On a Grassy Hill

desc:
You are on a path which leads you around the forest. Your steps are easy as the
terrain is not too rough. High and uninviting trees border the path all around,
you can see a few crows nests up in the branches.
name: A Path around the Forest

desc:
The little path you have been following is still there, but it seems to have
changed into something else. You try to see the way ahead of you but you find it
hard to concentrate. You feel you should enter the forest at some point but it
seems just too difficult from here.
name: A Path around the Forest

desc:
You are on a small path which is completely covered by the soft earth coming
from its border. The lack of tracks makes you wonder that this path is probably
not used during this season. From here the path goes east-west.
name: Small Earth Path

desc:
You are surrounded by a small forest. The forest seems to be growing in and
around a small range of hills. All around you are the songs of life which makes
you feel secure and at ease. To the north is a wide looking road compared to the
road, which has a woody border, in the east.
name: Forest in the Hills

desc:
You stand on a strip of clear path surrounded by woods. The path goes on in a
rather straight line for a long while. The woods seem actually quite nice, it
may be possible for you to find some firewood and start a nice cosy fire.
name: Woody Road

desc:
You are standing here close to the crossing of the Stock road and the woody path
heading south. You notice the forest looming to the south-east and the road
which leads east-to-west. Though you know that the path to the west goes towards
the south you cannot see it from here.
name: The Corner of Stock and Woody Roads

desc:
You are standing in between the woody path and the Woody End forest. The woods
to the south-east sure don't seem very inviting, actually you'd rather stay away
from there. You can see a trail of smoke heading to the skies from somewhere in
the middle of the forest. Wonder who lives there.
name: Close to the Road

desc:
There is a small well in the centre of this area. Its stone bricks have fallen
down and makes the area look a bad shape. As you peer down from the edge of the
well you notice some clear water at the bottom of it. You may be able to fill
your containers if you stretch out your hands and arms long enough.
name: A Deserted Well

desc:
You are standing on the edge of the huge forest of Woody End. One step north and
you could be on Stock Road, head south and you are swallowed into the forest.
The whole area seems peaceful with only the leaves swaying in the wind and the
occasional passers by.
name: Close to Woody End

desc:
This path which leads around the forest. Most travellers will find their
footsteps tread easily on the rather smooth terrain. Trees, high and uninviting,
border the path all around, a few crows nests can be seen up in the branches.
name: A Path around the Forest

desc:
You stand on a narrow path going through the forest, or around it. You could not
figure out which it was. Oaks, beeches and other great trees border the path.
Old fallen branches lay on the ground. Now and again you notice something in the
corner of your eye, however, when you turn or look you notice nothing.
name: Narrow Path in the Woody End

desc:
You are standing on the edge of the huge forest of Woody End, one step north and
you would stand on the Stock road, head south and you are swallowed into the
forest. The whole area seems peaceful with only the leaves swaying in the wind
and some occasional travellers walking by.
name: Close to Woody End

desc:
You stand in a really big field, all around you is grass and small trees, you
can see small yellow flowers and other plants. The field is flat and it seems to
serve as an eating place for animals.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in the middle of a little crossing. Trying to decide which way to go.
You catch a stench coming from the southern woods. The dark, hideous trees
southward do not seem very inviting at all, but still your sense of adventure
draws you to them.
name: Stock Road

desc:
The Stock Road continues its journey through the forests and eastern shire. The
forests are quiet and calm as you walk by. The sharp bushes around you allow you
to reconsider your exits. There is a small passage to the north.
name: Stock Road

desc:
The same old road continues to pierce the woods east and west. You are walking
at ease because you feel no threat around you. The forests seem calm and, well,
just normal. The leaves are swinging in the wind.
name: Stock Road

desc:
The forests surrounding this bit of the road have grown very close to the edge
of the road. There is little room between the tree barriers. You feel secure
because you can hop into the wood for cover whenever you want to.
name: Stock Road

desc:
The road is surrounded by healthy trees that seem well taken care of. As you
move on you see a wooded path leading southwards. The forest gets a little
darker the more southerly you try to see. The darkness seems to give the forest
an air of evil.
name: Stock Road

desc:
There is a huge stony archway above you. You can't imagine why it was put there
in the first place since it doesn't seem to have any permanent function,
although it provides a shady place of rest for a tired traveller. Some strange
substance is flowing through a hole to the north, and as you examine it further,
you notice it's some kind of slime.
name: Under an Archway

desc:
This appears to be a very old air ventilation shaft, you feel a soft breeze
coming up. As you climb down the ladders here your lantern provides enough light
to see all the bats hanging from the ceiling.
name: The Shaft

desc:
Your feet start to get wet from the water within the tunnel. The tunnel is now
almost half full of water. Now and then you feel some hideous creature touch
your knee. Rats! The place is filled with rats of various kinds. You can see
some scratches on the walls made by something a lot bigger than these annoying
rats.
name: Watery Tunnel

desc:
You are standing in the middle of a weasel's lair. It is dark and dank, smelling
of wet fur and the scent of dead prey. Soft twigs and half-decayed leaves cover
the walls with a mushy lining making it seem very comfortable.
name: Weasel's Lair

desc:
You are strolling along a middle-sized track or road which leads west and east.
All you can see ahead of you are many miles of roads and rows of trees and woods
bordering the road. This might be a good place to catch a little rest. The grass
is a little higher here and the area looks as if it were feeling rather well.
Some blueberry bushes lay on the side of the ditch.
name: Stock Road

desc:
You are strolling along a middle-sized track or road which leads west and east.
All you can see ahead of you are the miles and again miles of road and the woods
close to the road on the sides. This might be a good place to catch a little
rest. The grass is a little higher here and the area looks as if it were feeling
rather well. Some blueberry bushes lay on the side of the ditch.
name: Stock Road

desc:
The air seems quite stale and still. Rarely if ever can a bird be heard and
there is little evidence of any animals frequenting this spot. Whatever makes
this place unattractive to these creatures is certainly powerful.
name: Stony Woods

desc:
You are currently on the corner of the expanse of land that you see before you.
To the south is the road to Stock and to the west is grassland. The area has
trees sporadically placed unlike the ones you can see to the west.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
You are on the northern side of the Road to Stock. To the north is a clear
forest. The land is covered with grass and could possibly be a good area to feed
a horse or pony.
name: On the Side of the Road

desc:
You are wandering around a finely spaced forest. There are some scurrying
animals sniffing the ground and the berries that grow here and there. You can
hear the birds sing from the branches of the trees. The edge of the forest is
easily visible so you find it hard not to make your way out safely.
name: Clear Forest

desc:
As you walk along the road you suddenly think something is staring at you. The
staring seems to poke right through your spine and you hasten your pace to evade
whatever was glaring at you from the tree wall to the south.
name: Stock Road

desc:
Desolate describes this corner of the woods. Even the vegetation seems rather
lifeless and the trees look as if they are made of stone. The area is remarkably
quiet, lacking the sound of animals scurrying over the ground or even above
within the trees or sky.
name: Stony Woods

desc:
This is a place to which this hobbit pair has dedicated all of their lives.
There are some corn crops cultivated here. The couple will hopefully get a
decent harvest this year.
name: Growing Crops

desc:
This forest is made of hundreds of old oaks growing very close one to the other.
It is even difficult to see something between them, as they are building a true
dark wall, especially in the west. No birds songs can be heared and the soil is
naked, as no other plants can grow in the shadow of the canopy.
name: An Oak Forest

desc:
This orchard is made of several ranks of small apple trees. Obviously, some
hobbit is taking great care of it: the grass around the trees is carefully
cleaned of every foreign corpse, like dead leaves. Through the ranks, one can
see some fields in the north and the west, and some dark forest in the south and
the east.
name: An Orchard

desc:
This small hill provides a good view of the Shire. From here two dark lines can
be seen in the distant north. One is obviously the East Road and the other has
the reflective quality of a river. To the north-west the outline of several
towns can be seen and to the south the plain slowly slopes upwards until a small
hill is formed and consumed by a dense forest.
name: A Small Hill

desc:
Strangely, this field is greener than the others. Because the nearby fields form
a small hill, it is likely that much of the rain water collects here. Except for
the area that forms a narrow path to the north, the surrounding landscape is
hidden by the nearby hills which are covered by farmed fields.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
It is a very strange place. There is a big circle in the middle of a field.
There are some little mushrooms growing all around it. Obviously they have to be
edible because the farmer has preserved this place when he planted this part of
his field...
name: The Mushrooms' Place

desc:
The fields here are slowly sloping upwards and downwards, creating small hills
on the landscape. These peaceful fields are only limited by the small hills to
the far south. While the area all around is mostly covered by fields, a strange
spot can be seen in the far east.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
Like many of the fields in the Shire, this one is well maintained. The furrows,
running east-west, are quite parallel, and not a single weed can be found. The
Hobbits are meticulous farmers, working from sun up to sun down. To the far
south the plain begins to slope upwards forming some rolling green hills. To the
north fields cover the plain as far as the eye can see.
name: A Cultivated Field

desc:
Although there is a small path from the west, it seems that the farmers have
abandonded this field. For unlike the other fields of the Shire, this field is
no longer cultivated. With weeds growing wild, a small barrier has formed,
preventing movement towards the south.
name: Waste Field

desc:
The path winds through a field which is rarely used, as evident from the patches
of grass growing on it. Such a field is unusual in the country farmed by
hobbits. Usually the hobbits are very careful farmers, and catch all weeds
trying to invade their precious fields. The nearby fields confirm this
impression: this field is the only one not to be well-kept.
name: A North-South Path

desc:
This is getting very nasty indeed, the mud is crawling with little snakes and
other poisonous things which you don't actually want to see. Even thinking what
there might be makes you hasten your step to almost running--well what would be
running without all this mud.
name: On a Muddy Path

desc:
This is getting very nasty indeed, the mud is crawling with little snakes and
other poisonous things which you don't actually want to see. Even thinking what
there might be makes you fasten your step to almost running, well what would be
running without all this mud.
name: On a Muddy Path

desc:
Suddenly the fairly easily followed earth path becomes a muddy path, the mud
reaching your knees at best. Progressing is getting a lot harder step by step.
The mud doesn't look very nice, some nasty looking worms and other insects make
you feel sick, better keep your eyes forward and plunge on.
name: Following a Muddy Path

desc:
You stand on a small earth path with the high forests west and south of you. You
can sense the humidity of this place as if you could touch it. And the feeling
of being watched has never been greater, it feels as it pairs and again pairs of
invisible eyes were staring at you lusting for your blood.
name: Small Earth Path

desc:
You stand on a small earth path with the high forests west and south of you. You
can sense the humidity of this place as if you could touch it. And the feeling
of being watched has never been greater, it feels as it pairs and again pairs of
invisible eyes were staring at you lusting for your blood.
name: Small Earth Path

desc:
You stand on a small path which is completely covered by the soft muddy earth
coming from its sides. The lack of tracks makes you wonder if this path is
actually used at all at this season. Here you can follow the path west and
south.
name: Small Earth Path

desc:
You stand on a small path which is completely covered by the soft muddy earth
coming from its sides. The lack of tracks makes you wonder if this path is
actually used at all at this season. Here you can follow the path north and
east.
name: Small Earth Path

desc:
The forest looms all around you by now, footsteps of various kinds surround you
on this very small clearing very close to the path from which you entered to
woods. Already you feel that this wasn't the wisest decision you've made since
now, the woods don't actually seem very friendly.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above your head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. The trees have given way to make a little
clearing here. In the middle of the clearing is a cool, refreshing pond. This
looks like a nice place to rest before continuing on to find your way out of the
forest.
name: A Pond

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
The Stockbrook widens here and the current weakens. Not too far to the east, a
bridge can be seen, and beyond that, the River Baranduin. North of the bridge is
the small village of Stock.
name: The Stockbrook

desc:
You are on the Brandywine river. The river curves gently here from the east to
the south. To the north and west is the road called the Causeway. Not far beyond
the road, you can see a small town full of buildings. Perhaps, if you are tired,
you could stop by that town for a good, East Farthing beer.
name: Bend in the Brandywine

desc:
You are in the middle of the Brandywine river. The river is flowing gently from
north to south. To the west is the road called the Causeway. Beyond the road,
you can make out the Woody End forest.
name: The Brandywine

desc:
All is quiet and peaceful as you float here on the Brandywine river. Here, the
river traces a graceful curve from the north to the west. Following the course
of the river, to the northwest, is the Causeway. Beyond the road, you can make
out the forest called Woody End.
name: Bend in the Brandywine

desc:
You find it easy to daydream out here on the Brandywine. The river traces its
path from east to south with any disturbance. Alongside the river, to the north
and west, is the road called the Causeway. Beyond the road you can just make out
the Woody End forest.
name: Bend in the Brandywine

desc:
You are floating peacefully on the Brandywine river as it makes its way from
north to south. Off to the west, following the river, is the Causeway. Beyond
the Causeway, you can see the Woody End forest.
name: The Brandywine

desc:
You are floating on the Brandywine river. The river flows from the north to the
south here, but you don't feel that continuing south would be very interesting.
To the west, following the river, is the Causeway. Beyond the road, you can make
out the Woody End forest.
name: The Brandywine

desc:
Everything around is an immense field of thick grass. You can see some lonely
trees growing in the middle. To the west you can see a hobbit farm and its
cultivated fields. Colorful flowers add life to the scene.
name: Green Fields

desc:
In every direction you look, an endless field extends, covered by green and
thick grass. Lonely trees can be seen in different places and wild flowers of
mixed colors give some life to this land.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are now standing on one side of the road going to Stock. The field is still
around and you feel the grass under your feet. To the north the field continues
on.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are now standing in a huge field of thick grass that reaches your knees.
Around you, some colorful wildflowers give life to the view. To the south, you
can see the road going to Stock.
name: Green Fields

desc:
In every direction you look, an endless field extends, covered by green and
thick grass. Lonely trees can be seen in different places and wild flowers of
mixed colors give some life to this land.
name: Green Fields

desc:
You are standing in a fertile, yet boggy, area. You can see some deep Hobbit
footprints in the soft soil. Few Hobbits live here anymore, as most have
probably moved to Buckland. Across a brook to the northeast, you can see a
village.
name: The Marish

desc:
The lands around you are extremely boggy. Many footprints can be seen, deep
indentations in the soft soil. This wetland is difficult to travel, the mud and
wet weeds clinging to your feet, and the road which lies to the south offers an
easier route to follow.
name: The Marish

desc:
You are glad that you are on a solid road and do not have to walk through the
Marish. To the southeast, you can see the magnificent river of the Brandywine.
Far to the west, you can see the forest of Woody End. The road continues to the
east and south.
name: Bend in the Causeway

desc:
You are on a well-crafted road, ideally suited for travelling on. To the east,
you can see the magnificent river of the Brandywine. To the west, you can see a
lane; and in the distance, a little north of the lane, you see tall trees
planted in a circular pattern. The road continues to the north and south.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are on a well-crafted road that is ideally suited for travelling on. To the
east, you can see the magnificent river of the Brandywine. Far to the west, you
can see the forest of Woody End. The road continues to the north and west.
name: Bend in the Causeway

desc:
You are travelling on a well-crafted road alongside the Brandywine river to the
southeast. Far to the west, you can see the forest of Woody End. The road
continues to the south and east with a short path to an old shanty-like home to
the west. Off to the southwest you can see the village of Rushey.
name: Bend in the Causeway

desc:
Hanging on the walls of this small home, there are many magnificent paintings of
a mighty ship being tossed about by raging waves. The pictures all show the same
ship with the same stout captain at the wheel. The lone occupant of this home, a
withered captain, occasionally glances up at the paintings, losing his thoughts
seemingly for hours.
name: Old Captain's Home

desc:
You are inside Maggot's farm, standing in a wide, grassy yard. The worn path
from the gate splits into three smaller pathways. To the west is the house, and
to the east is a worn out barn. To the north, there is another building that
distinctly smells of dogs.
name: Maggot's Farm

desc:
This building is simply a big doghouse. The ceiling here is high enough that
anyone can walk in here without having to kneel down. The size of this place
suggests that more than one huge dog lives here.
name: Kennel

desc:
You are inside a secret tunnel. You wonder what a farmer like Old Maggot would
use a tunnel like this for. To the west, a barrel acts as a secret passage way
to the next room. To the east, you can see a sliding wall.
name: Barrel's Tunnel

desc:
This seems to be Farmer Maggot's secret cellar where he keeps his best beer. The
smell of ale dominates this small room giving you reason to believe he often
keeps this small room well stocked. There is also a barrel of wine, a rarity in
the Shire.
name: Cellar

desc:
You are in the living room of Farmer Maggot's house. The room is furnished with
comfortable furniture that looks inviting to your weary legs. To the west, there
is an archway that leads into a dining room dominated by a large elm table. Next
to the front door to the east is a stairway leading up to a hallway.
name: Living Room

desc:
This large, by Hobbit standards, room is dominated by a large, round elm dining
table. The table is large enough to seat many Hobbits, and is surrounded by a
like number of finely carved elm chairs.
name: Dining Room

desc:
You are in the kitchen of Farmer Maggot's house. On one side, there is a huge
stove which is used to cook the meals for the family and their guests. Lining
the rest of the walls, there are cupboards where various breads, mushrooms, and
vegetables are kept. Your nose twitches as you catch the scent of a finely
brewed beer.
name: Kitchen

desc:
You are in a bedroom. Judging by the size of the beds, this must be a room for
Hobbit children. There are fourteen little beds arranged in an orderly manner
about the room. A door leads east, out into the hallway.
name: Bedroom

desc:
You are standing in an upstairs hallway. To the south, there is a door that you
guess leads to Mr. Maggot's bedroom. To the west, there is a door leading to
another bedroom. A stairway along the north wall leads down to the living room.
name: Hallway

desc:
You have found Old Maggot's secret hideaway. All around you is dark. The walls
seem to be made of a strange material that seems to absorb light. You can just
make out a stout chest set in the centre of the room. This must be where Old
Maggot has stashed his fortune. Greed and morality war for control of your
actions as you gaze upon it.
name: Private Vault

desc:
You have entered an underwater cave, and it is even darker in here than it was
out in the pond. You feel along the walls, hoping to discover anything. It may
be your imagination, but there was a faint flicker of light from above. You
think you can make out a small hole in the ceiling that leads up to an air
filled space.
name: Underwater Cave

desc:
You have finally reached the muddy bottom of this pond. You are surrounded by
darkness. As your eyes adjust to the gloom, you can make out the oval-shaped
entrance of a cave to the north.
name: Bottom of a Pond

desc:
A small round cave opens up, its wall in some places made from packed earth, in
others by naked stone. A rough straw pallet lies at the base of one wall, while
a low wooden table bearing a single flickering candle sits near the centre of
the cave. The cave extends further to the north.
name: The Hermit's Cave

desc:
You are amazed to find a dry cave without the water from below rushing in. The
air in here is quite warm, despite the chill of the water below. Other than the
spiders and bugs that make their home here, this cave is actually quite
comfortable. The cavern continues to the west and south. To the south, you can
make out a faint flicker of light.
name: Dry Cave

desc:
You have entered a small cavern under the roots of the trees. You aren't sure
where you are, but you think you are near the surface. As you move about in this
cramped space, you catch a glimpse of an occasional glimmer of light from above.
The cavern widens out a bit to the east.
name: Hole in the Ceiling

desc:
Encircling you, the forest seems to proctect this small glade from the evils of
the world. An almost ideal sylvan setting, your surroundings soothe your mind
and remove its cares and worries. The ground is covered with ferns and moss,
cushioning your steps and inviting you to rest. A drowsy warm breeze floats
through, ruffling your hair and caressing your face.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
Graceful elms and doughty oaks surround you, preventing travel from this glade.
Yet your mind is soothed, not worried, and here you feel secure from the horror
and hatred of the war. In this one glade, perhaps alone in Middle-earth, the
breeze brings tidings of peace, and the birds sing care-free.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
The branches of the trees enclose this small spring, forming a natural roof
above your head. It is shady here, yet not too dark, and the shadows play in the
depths of the spring. Strangely it seems cooler here than in the rest of the
glade.
name: Secluded Spring

desc:
A mighty oak grows here, sprouting from the forest floor and reaching high
towards the forest canopy. Its trunk is wide and strong, and three grown men
could not encircle it with outstretched arms. The bark is of a deep brown
colour, and the forest floor is only invaded by a few stout roots. It looks
possible to ascend the tree from here.
name: Base of a Tree

desc:
The great oak tree has grown for many years, and has seen many changes to the
land. Still, and perhaps for ever, it spreads its branches about this space, and
boughs serve under your feet as a floor. The wind rustles the leaves, and
soothing murmurs come to you from the breeze. The limbs of the oak have grown
into such shapes that are comfortable to rest on, or recline under. The
peacefulness of this land pervades the very air which you breathe.
name: Natural Terrace

desc:
You enter the pond and dive under its surface. The water of this pond is very
cold. Looking down, you can see the muddy bottom and some fish that live there.
To the east, you see a dark, dangerous looking cavern.
name: Inside a Pond

desc:
The water is warmer in here. All you can see are curious white fish that are
swimming around in here. This cavern seems to be a dead end. You think you
should leave before you run out of air, or worse.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
You are standing on a well-crafted road that leads to the north. Alongside the
road, to the east, is the Brandywine river. Far off into the west, you can see
the forest of Woody End.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You pause to survey your surroundings while travelling on this well-crafted
road. To your east, you can see the magnificent Brandywine river. Far to the
west, you can see the forest of Woody End. The road continues to the north and
south of here.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are travelling on a well-crafted road alongside the Brandywine river to the
east. Far to the west, you can make out the Woody End forest. The road here
continues to the north and south.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are travelling on a well-crafted road along side the Brandywine river to the
east. To the west, you can see the small Hobbit village of Rushey. The road
continues north and south of here.
name: The Causeway

desc:
You are on a well-crafted road, ideally suited for travelling. To the east, you
can see the magnificent Brandywine river. Far to the west, you can see the
forest of Woody End. Closer, to the northwest, you can see the village of
Rushey. The road here continues to the north and west.
name: Bend in the Causeway

desc:
You are on a well-crafted road that is ideally suited for travelling. To the
southeast flows the magnificent Brandywine river. Far to the west, you can see
the forest of Woody End. To the north, but much closer, is the village of
Rushey. The road continues to the south and east.
name: Bend in the Causeway

desc:
You see the village grocer standing behind a finely crafted, hand-built counter.
Arrayed behind and on the counter are some delicious mushrooms along with other
miscellaneous items that the grocer has for sale.
name: Mushroom Market

desc:
You have arrived at the central square of the village of Rushey. All around you,
you can see Hobbits walking in and out of their Hobbit houses. Though these
Hobbits do not live in holes, you can still see the curves and circles of
Hobbit-style architecture in their doors and windows. There is a market to the
north, the River Inn to the south, and an ancient-looking Hobbit house to the
west.
name: Rushey

desc:
This is the southern end of the central square of Rushey, a small village of
Hobbits built near the Brandywine's western bank. Flanked by several
Hobbit-houses, the River Inn lies directly to the south; a pleasant scent of
stew and baked mushrooms wafts out of the door. A more recent building can be
seen to the west.
name: In Front of the River Inn

desc:
The first thing you notice upon entering this home are the beautiful paintings
and sculptures decorating this home. You wonder if the owner of this home is a
very wealthy Hobbit. Upon looking at the furniture, which is more practical than
luxurious, you reconsider.
name: A Wise Old Hobbit's Home

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. The trees have given way to make a little
clearing here. In the middle of the clearing is a cool, refreshing pond. This
looks like a nice place to rest before continuing on to find your way out of the
forest.
name: A Pond

desc:
This is a small path going in north-south direction across the plain. Cultivated
land surrounds the path in every direction with a board at the entrance to the
east field. At some distance to the south, the ground slopes gently upwards
until it reaches some woody hills.
name: A North-South Path

desc:
The ground begins to slope upwards and the first trees of a dense forest, which
covers the small mountain to the south, can be seen here. To the north there are
some fields and to the west the remains of the path disappears.
name: Entrance of a Forest

desc:
This is a very well maintained field. Usually hobbits fields are well-kept but
this one is really carefully maintained. It is covered with parallel lines of
plush green plants with large leaves. A tobacco smell fills the place.
name: A Pipe-weed Field

desc:
The newly built stables of Rushey stand freshly painted just west of the old
River Inn. The structure is made out of logs with a thatched roof to keep the
worst rains out and several large boxes has been created to allow a number of
mounts to be housed while their owner visits the tavern. A small plaque can be
seen next to the exit.
name: Stables of the River Inn

desc:
You are in the common room of the River Inn. The merry Hobbits of Rushey come
and go, laughing, singing, and drinking mugs of beer. Against the far wall,
there is a bar where you can order a beer for yourself. A stairway leads
upstairs where you can rent a room for a night or two.
name: River Inn

desc:
You have found the innkeeper of the River Inn. As you step into the room, your
feet throw up a cloud of dust. You start to wonder if this innkeeper ever sweeps
the floor. As you are wondering this, you notice a trail in the dust next to the
desk.
name: The River Inn's Reception

desc:
You manage to squeeze through the opening behind the desk. There are no windows
or doors, except for the opening you just came through. Judging by the secrecy
of this room, you think that the innkeeper must keep some very valuable things
in here.
name: Secret Desk

desc:
This small, well maintained field is one of many to be found on the plain of the
Shire. As all well maintained fields are, this one is covered by cultivated
plants. Amidst this peaceful land there are some towns to the north and a nearby
woody hill nearby to the south.
name: Well-farmed Field

desc:
You are surrounded by rolling hills, all covered in lush green grass. This
entire area is perfect for grazing livestock, and that is exactly what it is
being used for. You can see a few odd brown spots where the livestock has eaten
the grass through to the soil. To the north the rolling hills continue; in all
the other directions the terrain appears to be more level.
name: Rolling Pasture-Land

desc:
You stand on a narrow path going through the forest, or around it. You could not
figure out which it was. Oaks, beeches and other great trees border the path.
Old fallen branches lay on the ground. Now and again you notice something in the
corner of your eye, however, when you turn or look you notice nothing.
name: Narrow Path in the Woody End

desc:
You have entered a clearing between the woody path and the forest. The grass has
had the chance to grow tall here and the place has its own atmosphere. The
peacefulness of the place is sometimes bothered by animal bellows from the
eastern woods. Otherwise there is nothing else here, but you can see some ruins
to the south.
name: A Tiny Forest Clearing

desc:
Passing through the open fields, the path and the surrounding environment
harmoniously converge. A few small boulders line the trail, seemingly organized
by a caring hand. To the north the fields open up even more, looking profoundly
tranquil.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
Leading through a vast field, the path goes in east-west direction. Here and
there the trail zig-zags a little, going around larger boulders or the
occasional grove of trees.
name: Path to Stock

desc:
You are in the famous forest of Woody End. There are many trees around you which
block and limit your view to within a few paces. Most of the trees are oaks and
beeches, heavy vegetation is apparent on the floor and the density of the
humidity is such that you sense you could almost touch it.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
A sense of warmth and appetizing aromas fill the room, inviting one to sit on a
comfortable-looking sofa located in front of a brick fireplace. Above the
fireplace and along the walls are a cluster of colourful paintings, and pegs to
hang coats and hats. A round window gives a beautiful view to the south: a
hillside filled with snapdragons and sunflowers slopes down towards the clear
waters of the gently flowing Stockbrook.
name: Josie's Smial

desc:
You stand in a forest; the many trees around you restrict your view to a few
paces. Most of the trees are oaks and beeches, and heavy vegetation covers the
ground. The humidity is so intense you can almost touch it.
name: Woody Path

desc:
You are making your way through high vegetation when you suddenly realise there
is a passage to the forest from here. The wall of trees to the east blocks the
passage and view. Not much light could ever pass through those leaves. You can
enter the woody end forest to the east or keep following the road.
name: Woody Intersection

desc:
You are making your way through high and dense vegetation when you suddenly
realise there is a passage to the forest from here. The wall of trees to the
west prevents the passage and blocks out the view. Not much light filters
through the leaves. You can enter the wooded end of the forest to the east or
continue to follow the road.
name: Woody Path

desc:
The various paths around and through the woods are all coming to a junction
right here, the east-westward paths are very clearly on sight but the ones going
south are quickly swallowed by the trees. You can barely see the Stock Road to
the north. There is some structure above the trees there too.
name: A Woody Crossing

desc:
A proper hobbit hole it is, cosy, well lit, and of course tidy as ever. Porthole
sized windows are dotted about the walls and well polished wood makes up the
floor. Plush, decorated rugs lie underfoot while up above a tiny chandelier
dangles from the ceiling. It looks like an good place to rest one's tired feet!
name: Home of the Metal Halfling

desc:
What once was a path is wholly covered with mud. It seems to be really thick and
a few bubbles on it indicate that it should not be hard at all. The rest of a
dead stag, mostly some bones carefully washed by ants, can be seen. The poor
animal was obviously asphyxiated when its head was caught in the mud. All around
is a thick forest, with sharp bushes, preventing almost every move, except
towards the northern path.
name: Muddy Hole

desc:
The bedroom is spacious and inviting, constructed of rich wood paneling that
creates a cozy and luxurious atmosphere. At the center of the room stands a
large four-poster bed constructed from mahogany and draped in burgundy-colored
silk curtains. Soft, plush pillows and a thick duvet cover the luxurious bed. A
beautiful fireplace made of aged stone radiates welcoming warmth. Two plush
armchairs and a small table are arranged around the fireplace, offering a
comfortable seating area for reading or relaxing by the fire. Like the rest of
the home, the bedroom is well-appointed and tastefully decorated. In one corner
sits a large wooden wardrobe adorned with intricate carvings. Within the
wardrobe are several leather pouches and wooden boxes, each filled with trinkets
and tiny treasures from faraway lands.
name: Luxurious Bedchamber

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
You are in the Woody End forest. Many elm trees tower above you head, blocking
out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard to travel
because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. Everywhere you turn, you
see trees and more trees. You seem to be travelling in circles.
name: Woody End Forest

desc:
A bloody mixture of stench and bad air almost makes you faint. There are some
bare skulls and other bones lying in the corners of this chamber. Some little
scaled things crawl around here but there are also some large footsteps in the
ground.
name: The Serpents' Hole

desc:
You've entered an underground chamber of stone. There is not much to this place,
except stones of different types and sizes all around. How they all got here is
a mystery. Maybe someone has carried them all here - who knows.
name: Stony Chamber
